article_title: Final Fantasy XIV Reveals Evercold for January 2027 With a Twin-Mode Battle System Overhaul, an Evangelion Raid, and the Game's First New Saga in 13 Years
summary: Strong gaming-news piece structurally, but multiple specifics are not in the cited Kotaku snapshots: (1) Yoshida quote 'largest structural shift since A Realm Reborn' — not in any source ('A Realm Reborn' is never named); (2) the EVANGELION raid subtitle 'Ghosts of Desire', the '24-player' alliance-raid count, and the 'khara, Inc.' partner attribution — none of these appear in source-1, which only describes a 'collab with Neon Genesis Evangelion, made up of a series of raids'; (3) 'Anaheim' is not in any snapshot; (4) 'Patch 8.0 / level cap 100→110' — only '110' is stated, not the '100' baseline nor 'Patch 8.0'; (5) several supporting specifics are not in the cited sources ('job identity' phrasing, 'outside combat', 'viable in high-end content', 'years-in-making', 'install size'). Title is also above the 150-char ceiling (154).

findings.message: Yoshida quote 'largest structural shift since A Realm Reborn' is not in any cited source
findings.details: Neither 'A Realm Reborn' nor any 'structural shift' phrasing appears in source-0, source-1, or source-2. Either source separately or rewrite without attributing this framing to Yoshida.

findings.message: EVANGELION raid: subtitle 'Ghosts of Desire', '24-player', and 'khara, Inc.' are not in source-1
findings.details: Source-1 only says 'collab with Neon Genesis Evangelion, made up of a series of raids' — no subtitle, no player-count, no partner studio name. The article claims 'an alliance raid series titled EVANGELION — Ghosts of Desire, a 24-player crossover developed in collaboration with anime studio khara, Inc.' All three specifics are unsourced. Drop or re-source each.

findings.message: 'Anaheim' is not in any cited source
findings.details: The article asserts the Fan Festival was in Anaheim. None of the 3 Kotaku snapshots mention Anaheim. Drop the location or cite an additional source.

findings.message: 'Patch 8.0' and 'level cap from 100 to 110' partially unsourced
findings.details: Source-1 says 'max level is going up to 110' — the previous cap (100) and the patch number (8.0) are not in source-1. The 8.0 patch number does appear elsewhere in source-1 only as '8.3' / '7.56' references. Either drop '100' (the baseline) or attribute it to general FFXIV knowledge, and confirm 'Patch 8.0' from a Square Enix primary source.

findings.message: Several specifics not in cited sources
findings.details: Article-vs-snapshot mismatches: (a) 'job identity' phrasing — not in source-1 (which says 'fewer inputs' / 'streamlined'); (b) 'players can swap between the two modes freely outside combat without changing gear or level' — source-1 says 'select between... at any time' and 'doesn't require new gear or levels' but not 'outside combat'; (c) 'both will remain viable in high-end content' — not stated; (d) 'years in the making' for Evangelion partnership — not stated; (e) PS4 'citing the expansion's growing install size as the trigger' — source-1 says size is growing, not 'install size' literally; (f) 'differs from the model Microsoft applies to Xbox' — Xbox comparison is not in source-2. Each is minor individually but cumulatively the article asserts more than the sources support.

Verbatim/clean: 'Godless Realms Saga' as first new arc in 13 years; jobs to be revealed at Berlin/Tokyo Fan Fests; Beastmaster in 7.56; future jobs ship only with Evolved Mode; setting is 'the Fourth' frozen reflection; Switch 2 in August 2026 with free month early access; Yoshida 'kind of freaked out' Nintendo + Limsa Lominsa demo; separate Switch 2 license + half-price subscription; 'many months of discussion with Nintendo' verbatim; Yoshida PS4/PS5 quote verbatim; PS4 retired after Patch 8.3; NA data-center boundaries removed.

content_preview.title: Final Fantasy XIV Reveals Evercold for January 2027 With a Twin-Mode Battle System Overhaul, an Evangelion Raid, and the Game's First New Saga in 13 Years
content_preview.summary: At Fan Festival 2026 in Anaheim, Square Enix announced FFXIV's sixth expansion Evercold, a Switch 2 launch in August, Reborn and Evolved combat modes, and an Evangelion alliance raid co-developed with khara, Inc.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Square Enix used the keynote of Final Fantasy XIV Fan Festival 2026 in Anaheim to unveil the MMO's sixth expansion, *Evercold*, alongside a battery of changes that producer and director Naoki Yoshida framed as the largest structural shift since *A Realm Reborn*. The expansion is scheduled to launch in January 2027 and will open the *Godless Realms Saga*, [the first new long-form story arc in the game's 13-year history according to Kotaku](https://kotaku.com/final-fantasy-14-evercold...

recommendations: Remove or re-source the 'A Realm Reborn structural shift' Yoshida framing.
Drop the 'Ghosts of Desire' subtitle, the '24-player' specifier, and the 'khara, Inc.' attribution unless an additional source supports them.
Drop 'Anaheim' or cite a separate source.
Reword the cluster of specifics that overstate the snapshots ('job identity', 'outside combat', 'viable in high-end content', 'years in the making', 'install size', 'vs Xbox model').

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong Gaming News piece (~879 words; over the News 1200-word ceiling but acceptable). Clear structure (Overview / What Was Announced / Switch 2 / Other 8.0 Changes / What We Don't Know / Why It Matters), neutral tone, good context-setting on FFXIV's longevity.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 3 Kotaku snapshots. Source-0 (expansion announcement) verifies: January 2027 launch, Godless Realms Saga as first new long-form arc in 13 years, 'the Fourth' frozen reflection setting, decade-plus single-saga framing through Dawntrail. Source-1 (jobs/raids) verifies: max level 110, two new non-limited jobs (tank + physical ranged DPS), Berlin/Tokyo job reveals, Beastmaster in 7.56, 21 jobs with Reborn (current) and Evolved (streamlined) modes, swap at any time, no new gear/level, future jobs only Evolved, Evangelion collab as raid series, Yoshida 'massive Evangelion otaku' verbatim, PS4 quote 'If you're still playing on a PS4, you've got two years to save up for a PS5' verbatim, PS4 retired after 8.3, NA data-center boundaries removed, color picker, Seasons system. Source-2 (Switch 2) verifies: Kiryu announcement, August launch, free month early access, Limsa Lominsa demo, Nintendo 'kind of freaked out a little', separate Switch 2 license, half-price subscription for active subscribers, no Switch Online required, 'many months of discussion with Nintendo' verbatim. NOT in any source: 'Anaheim'; 'A Realm Reborn'; 'structural shift'; 'Ghosts of Desire' raid subtitle; '24-player' count; 'khara, Inc.' partner; 'Patch 8.0' explicit number; 'level cap from 100' baseline; 'job identity' phrasing; 'outside combat' qualifier on mode swap; 'high-end content viability' framing; 'years in the making'; 'install size' literal phrase; Xbox comparison.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: About 60% of detailed claims verify cleanly. The unsupported items skew toward fabricated specifics (a Yoshida quote that isn't in the sources; a raid subtitle and 24-player count that aren't there; a partner studio name 'khara, Inc.' not present). These are not minor wording issues — they assert detail the sources do not contain.
concerns: Multiple invented specifics presented as sourced facts.
recommendations: Five fixes per findings — most are mechanical removals; the Evangelion raid paragraph needs the most rework.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid structural piece on a major MMO announcement, but the discipline of asserting only what the sources contain has slipped. Drop the unsourced specifics, trim the title, and the article will be cleanly approvable.
